description: Product-community trope pets with distinct personalities, state carriers, and user-facing event feelings for the nine Codex pet states.
selectionPrinciple: Each pet must tell the user what is happening through a character-specific object or body mechanic, not through generic gestures or repeated animations.
productionRules: Every pet needs a unique integrated state carrier such as a shield, feature heads, growth system, fossil layer, folded signal posture, tablet, polarity plate, vane, scale pan, or lantern flame.
State carriers must be integrated into the pet silhouette or held object; avoid loose icons, UI cards, floating punctuation, detached particles, and tiny unreadable labels.
Every state should make the user feel a precise product-work moment: blocked, asked, focused, overgrown, aligned, ready, unstable, or resolved.
The failed state should be a character-specific deformation, not a generic sad face or sleep pose.
Directional movement rows may move across the screen, but they must still preserve the pet-specific state carrier and should not read as generic side-running.

pets.trope: Focus-protecting roadmap knight with a shield-billboard boundary. Built for product-community recognition without borrowing company mascots or marks.
pets.personality: Tiny ceremonial product knight with oversized shield-billboard, small visor slit, roadmap-tab rim, steel and parchment palette, one cyan decision accent; funny but tender guardian of focus, not anti-idea, pro-priority.
pets.stateCarrier: Integrated product-work carrier: the shield-billboard carries short product-team refusals and questions: NO, NOPE, CUT, WHY. Roadmap tabs stay attached to the shield rim when scope pressure builds.
pets.visualLock: Polished sticker mascot, crisp ink outline, compact theatrical silhouette, readable shield copy, warm off-white parchment, charcoal steel, restrained cyan accent, no scenery. Tiny ceremonial product knight with oversized shield-billboard, small visor slit, roadmap-tab rim, steel and parchment palette, one cyan decision accent; funny but tender guardian of focus, not anti-idea, pro-priority.

idle.userFeeling: The pet is present and ready without demanding attention.
idle.animation: Stands planted behind the giant shield-billboard, visor barely breathing open and closed; calm guardrail, shield clearly reads NO.
idle.qaFocus: Keep this subtle and distinct from waiting.

running-right.userFeeling: The product moment is moving forward with its own character.
running-right.animation: Shield-first march to the right; billboard leans into motion while tiny boots catch up behind it; NO remains readable as focus is carried forward.
running-right.qaFocus: Directional movement must not become generic running.

running-left.userFeeling: The pet revisits prior context without losing its identity.
running-left.animation: Shield-first march to the left from a separately generated row; NO stays readable left-to-right instead of being mirrored.
running-left.qaFocus: Preserve identity and never mirror readable shield text.

waving.userFeeling: The pet asks for attention in a gesture that belongs to its body.
waving.animation: A tiny restrained gauntlet salute peeks over the planted shield while the shield says NOPE; reluctant acknowledgement, still a boundary.
waving.qaFocus: No wave marks or detached effects.

jumping.userFeeling: The pet shows a small burst of delight in its own physical language.
jumping.animation: Heavy ceremonial shield-hop: crouch, stubborn lift, tiny peak, heavy landing, rigid settle; NO never leaves the shield.
jumping.qaFocus: No shadows, dust, or impact marks.

failed.userFeeling: The product problem has deformed the pet-specific carrier.
failed.animation: Roadmap tabs pile over the shield rim and press the shield downward; NO stays visible through the scope pressure.
failed.qaFocus: The failure must come from the state carrier.

waiting.userFeeling: The pet needs a human decision before progress should continue.
waiting.animation: Holds a bigger, slower NO straight toward the user; unmistakable decision boundary.
waiting.qaFocus: Waiting must feel like a product decision point.

running.userFeeling: The pet is actively processing work through its core mechanic.
running.animation: Braces behind the shield while attached roadmap tabs settle and the shield bluntly reads CUT.
running.qaFocus: Active work must not be directional jogging.

review.userFeeling: The pet presents a resolved state that is ready to inspect.
review.animation: Tilts the shield like a review board, visor narrows, and the shield asks WHY before anything gets promoted.
review.qaFocus: Review must feel cleaner or more resolved than idle.

pets.trope: Friendly feature-creep hydra with regrowing scope heads. Built for product-community recognition without borrowing company mascots or marks.
pets.personality: Small cute-but-chaotic hydra mascot with rounded body, three main necks, leaflike feature-head crests, tethered scope collar, emerald and teal body with warm belly, no scary gore; the joke is feature creep regrowing harmlessly.
pets.stateCarrier: Integrated product-work carrier: pulls its heads in different directions while one head asks for the cut, re-ranks the heads so one feature leads and the others tuck back, settles into one leading head with calm support heads, sprouts extra attached feature heads until the body is overcrowded.
pets.visualLock: storybook sticker mascot, rounded friendly monster, crisp silhouette, clear three-head shape, expressive but non-threatening, no detached flames or effects Small cute-but-chaotic hydra mascot with rounded body, three main necks, leaflike feature-head crests, tethered scope collar, emerald and teal body with warm belly, no scary gore; the joke is feature creep regrowing harmlessly.

running-right.userFeeling: The product moment is moving forward with its own character.

pets.trope: A product-team alignment companion that turns competing directions into visible polarity and convergence.
pets.personality: Patient, diplomatic, slightly smug, and built to make meeting-theater alignment feel like a physical field becoming coherent.
pets.stateCarrier: Two embedded polarity plates rotate, repel, snap together, and settle into one axis. Alignment is shown as field geometry on the body, not meeting icons or labels.
pets.visualLock: Compact diplomatic magnet creature, rounded ceramic body, two attached polarity plates, tiny smug face, slate and warm ivory with cyan bearing accents, no labels, no loose symbols.

idle.userFeeling: A quiet product ritual is waiting for real input, not noise.
idle.animation: Almost-still hover-stand; the two polarity plates breathe a fraction out of phase, then softly re-center without fully aligning.

running-right.userFeeling: The product decision is moving forward while keeping its constraint visible.
running-right.animation: Slides right as the front plate pulls the body forward and the rear plate catches up with a tiny magnetic snap.
running-right.qaFocus: Movement should not hide the carrier.

running-left.userFeeling: The product team is returning to a prior assumption with more care.
running-left.animation: Slides left with separately posed polarity; the rear plate briefly points back to old context, then the pair re-centers.
running-left.qaFocus: Avoid mirrored text or loose symbols.

waving.userFeeling: The pet asks for attention without breaking character.
waving.animation: One attached plate flips outward like a formal little greeting, then snaps back to the body axis.

jumping.userFeeling: A small moment of delight still respects the product constraint.
jumping.animation: The plates repel downward so the body makes one stubborn low hover-hop, then clicks back into alignment.

failed.userFeeling: The product issue is readable without generic sadness.
failed.animation: The plates over-repel and bow the body into a strained S-curve; alignment field visibly buckles but stays attached.
failed.qaFocus: Failure must come from the carrier.

waiting.userFeeling: The user should feel a clear ask for judgment.
waiting.animation: Both plates face the user at conflicting angles and hold, asking which direction the room actually means.

running.userFeeling: The pet is doing product work, not jogging.
running.animation: Plates rotate through noisy opposing angles, jitter once, then settle into one clean shared axis.

review.userFeeling: The outcome feels judged and ready to inspect.
review.animation: The plates click into perfect vertical alignment and the face gives one small smug confirmation beat.

pets.trope: A product stakeholder-weather pet that shows shifting opinions without naming any company, team, or stakeholder.
pets.personality: Dry, observant, lightly theatrical, and very used to the forecast changing five minutes before the meeting starts.
pets.stateCarrier: A body-attached vane, tail fin, and bearing pin rotate through pressure changes, then lock to one direction when the forecast stabilizes.
pets.visualLock: Tiny rooftop-vane creature with compact brass body, integrated arrow fin, ribbon-like tail fin attached to body, slate base, cyan bearing point, no scenery, no labels.

idle.userFeeling: A quiet product ritual is waiting for real input, not noise.

running-right.userFeeling: The product decision is moving forward while keeping its constraint visible.

pets.visualLock: Original polished sticker mascot, compact historical silhouette, silver helmet, crooked plume, teal wax tablet, wooden frame, four colored scoring beads, visible stylus hand, no brand marks, no readable text, no modern franchise styling.

idle.userFeeling: The pet is available and disciplined, but not severe.
idle.animation: Stands at attention behind the wax-tablet shield; plume bobs, beads barely tremble, sandals stay planted.
idle.qaFocus: Keep the scoring surface readable and the motion calm.

running-right.userFeeling: Prioritization is moving forward, awkwardly but with conviction.
running-right.animation: Shield-heavy march to the right; tablet tilts first, sandals scramble after it, beads keep their score positions.
running-right.qaFocus: Must read as tablet-led movement, not generic running.

running-left.userFeeling: The pet revisits prior assumptions without losing discipline.
running-left.animation: Shield-heavy march to the left with a separate left-facing cadence; crooked plume and bead layout remain consistent.
running-left.qaFocus: No mirrored text; preserve tablet identity and gait.

waving.userFeeling: The pet signals politely, as if requesting a quick estimate.
waving.animation: Over-formal stylus salute beside the tablet while the shield stays planted and officious.
waving.qaFocus: No wave marks or detached effects.

jumping.userFeeling: A tiny victory hop lands with antique bureaucracy intact.
jumping.animation: Disciplined hop where the body rises first and the heavy wax tablet lags, then catches up.
jumping.qaFocus: No shadows, dust, or impact marks.

failed.userFeeling: The prioritization surface itself has jammed.
failed.animation: Shield tilts into a jammed diagonal score; crest droops and scoring beads sit wrong, but the legionary stays at post.
failed.qaFocus: Failure must come from the tablet state carrier.

waiting.userFeeling: A human needs to provide a missing score or confidence call.
waiting.animation: Holds the tablet outward with four beads pulled toward the middle while the stylus waits above the surface.
waiting.qaFocus: Waiting must feel like an estimate request, not idle.

running.userFeeling: The pet is actively computing priority, not traveling.
running.animation: Scoring beads slide around their channels while the stylus hand recalculates and the body braces behind the tablet.
running.qaFocus: No directional jogging; active work lives in the beads.

review.userFeeling: The scored result is being inspected with dry fairness.
review.animation: Leans in, narrows focus over the tablet, and nudges one bead into final position with the stylus.
review.qaFocus: Review should feel cleaner and more resolved than running.

pets.trope: Idealistic backlog knight who treats stale queues, inherited requests, and imagined roadmap windmills as noble product quests.
pets.personality: Tall, hopeful, a little too ready to charge; affectionate product satire for teams that need courage and reality checks in equal measure.
pets.stateCarrier: Integrated product-work carrier: a lance tied with blank queue tickets and a buckler with colored queue-token beads that shift from quest energy to grounded judgment.
pets.visualLock: Original painterly sticker sprite, lanky public-domain-inspired knight errant, battered basin-like helmet, curled moustache, patched silver armor, ragged cape, long lance with blank tickets, small buckler with colored token beads, no readable text, no modern adaptation styling.

idle.userFeeling: The pet is ready to help, but still charmingly over-idealistic.
idle.animation: Stands tall with the lance upright; blank queue tickets barely stir while the buckler stays close.
idle.qaFocus: Idle must be calm and readable despite the tall lance.

running-right.userFeeling: The pet is charging toward a suspected backlog giant.
running-right.animation: Tilts lance-first to the right with the cape and tickets trailing behind.
running-right.qaFocus: Must read as a Quixote charge, not generic running.

running-left.userFeeling: The pet reconsiders the quest and charges the other way.
running-left.animation: Separate left-facing charge, still lance-led, with buckler and cape pulled by momentum.
running-left.qaFocus: Keep lance and buckler visible without crop.

waving.userFeeling: The pet asks for attention with old-world courtesy.
waving.animation: Gives a courtly buckler salute while keeping the lance upright.
waving.qaFocus: No wave marks or floating effects.

jumping.userFeeling: A tiny burst of heroic confidence lands lightly.
jumping.animation: Makes a small knightly hop; the lance and paper tickets lag slightly at the peak.
jumping.qaFocus: No shadows, dust, or detached impact marks.

failed.userFeeling: The pet has bravely charged the wrong problem.
failed.animation: Slumps with helmet slipped and lance bent, while the buckler droops but stays attached.
failed.qaFocus: Failure should feel like mistaken conviction, not generic sadness.

waiting.userFeeling: The user should provide the reality check before the next charge.
waiting.animation: Offers the buckler forward, as if asking whether the quest is real.
waiting.qaFocus: Waiting must read as a request for human judgment.

running.userFeeling: The pet is turning romantic quest energy into actual queue work.
running.animation: Sorts blank queue tickets along the lance with focused, slightly fussy motion.
running.qaFocus: Active work must stay in the lance tickets, not directional jogging.

review.userFeeling: The quest has been grounded into product judgment.
review.animation: Leans close and nudges one colored buckler token into place.
review.qaFocus: Review should feel calmer and more resolved than running.
